constraints.reasoningTemp: Reasoning models use a fixed temperature of 1.0. This parameter will be ignored by the API.

tooltips.temperature: Controls randomness in responses. Low (0-0.3): precise, deterministic outputs. High (0.7-1.0): more creative and varied responses.
tooltips.topP: Alternative to temperature. Only considers tokens whose cumulative probability exceeds this threshold. 0.1 = very focused, 1.0 = full vocabulary.
tooltips.frequencyPenalty: Reduces repetition by penalizing tokens already frequent in the output. Positive (0.5-1.0) discourages verbatim repetition. Range: -2 to 2.
tooltips.presencePenalty: Encourages topic diversity. Penalizes each token that has appeared, regardless of frequency. Positive = explores more topics. Range: -2 to 2.
tooltips.maxTokens: Maximum number of output tokens. Limits the length of the generated response. Optimal value depends on the agent type.
tooltips.timeout: Maximum wait time in seconds before aborting the API call. Leave empty to use the provider's default.
tooltips.reasoningEffort: Controls the model's internal thinking intensity. Higher = better accuracy but increased latency and cost. Lower = fast responses for simple tasks.

skills.title: LIA Capabilities (skills)
skills.description: Skills extend the assistant's capabilities with expert instructions and specialized workflows.
skills.empty: No imported skills. Import SKILL.md files to add new capabilities.
skills.admin_description: Skills shipped with the application. Read-only.
skills.desc_briefing-quotidien: Generates a comprehensive morning briefing combining calendar events, priority tasks, and weather forecast. Use when the user asks for a daily briefing or morning summary.
skills.desc_redaction-professionnelle: Provides expert guidance for writing professional emails, reports, and messages with appropriate tone, structure, and formulas. Use for formal communications or business correspondence.
skills.desc_synthese-recherche: Guides structured web research with source cross-referencing and critical synthesis. Use when the user needs in-depth research or comprehensive analysis of a topic.
skills.desc_preparation-reunion: Prepares meeting materials by gathering calendar details, participant contacts, and recent email history. Use when the user mentions preparing for a meeting.
skills.desc_coaching-productivite: Provides productivity coaching with prioritization frameworks (Eisenhower, Pomodoro) and habit-building strategies. Use when the user asks for help organizing tasks or managing time.
skills.guide_format: .md file: a single SKILL.md file with a YAML header (name, description) followed by Markdown instructions.
skills.guide_zip: .zip file: a compressed folder containing SKILL.md and optionally sub-folders (references/, scripts/, assets/).
skills.guide_frontmatter: The YAML header must start with --- and contain at least the name and description fields.
skills.guide_marketplace: Compatible with agentskills.io standard skills (skillsmp.com, GitHub, etc.).
skills.translate_button_label: Translate description of {{name}} to all languages
skills.delete_admin_confirm_description: Skill "{{name}}" will be permanently deleted from the system. This action cannot be undone.
skills.edit_description_hint: Write the description in your language. It will be automatically translated to English (stored in SKILL.md) and all 6 interface languages.
skills.guide_modal_what_body: A skill is a SKILL.md file that gives the assistant expert instructions for a specific domain. The assistant reads these instructions and applies them automatically when relevant — without you having to repeat the context each time.
skills.guide_modal_format_intro: A SKILL.md file starts with a YAML header (between ---) containing metadata, followed by a Markdown body with the detailed instructions.
skills.guide_modal_struct_body: For complex skills, import a .zip file containing SKILL.md and sub-folders: references/ for reference documents, scripts/ for executable Python scripts, assets/ for templates and resources.
skills.guide_modal_tip_1: Write the description in the third person: "Generates...", "Provides...", "Guides..."
skills.guide_modal_tip_2: The description should state what the skill does AND when to use it (the trigger).
skills.guide_modal_tip_3: Keep instructions concise and structured — prefer numbered lists.
skills.guide_modal_tip_4: Name your skill in lowercase without spaces: use my-skill (kebab-case).
skills.guide_modal_plan_body: The plan_template defines a sequence of tools executed automatically, bypassing the LLM planner. Ideal for recurring, predictable workflows. Steps without depends_on run in parallel.
skills.guide_modal_plan_field_agent: Agent name to use (see table below)
skills.guide_modal_plan_agents: Available agents and their main tools:
skills.guide_modal_plan_note: Each agent has additional tools (create, update, delete…). Only read tools are listed here.
skills.guide_modal_compat_body: Skills are 100% compatible with the agentskills.io ecosystem — the same format is used by Claude Code, Cursor, VS Code, GitHub Copilot and 30+ other products. Import skills from marketplaces like skillsmp.com.
skills.guide_modal_link: Learn more about the agentskills.io standard

intro.description: LIA is a next-generation AI assistant that combines advanced technologies to offer you an intelligent, secure, and personalized experience.

architecture.title: 🏗️ LangGraph Multi-Agent Architecture
architecture.description: LIA uses LangGraph, a state graph framework for AI agent orchestration. 16 specialized domain agents (contacts, email, calendar, drive, tasks, weather, wikipedia, perplexity, brave, web_search, web_fetch, places, routes, reminders, hue, context, query) + dynamic MCP agents, each with conditional transitions and state persistence via PostgreSQL checkpointing. Dynamic MCP agents extend capabilities without limit.

queryAnalyzer.title: 🧭 Query Analyzer v3.2
queryAnalyzer.description: Unified LLM query analysis (P95 < 800ms): intent detection (action/conversation), multi-domain routing with confidence, English normalization, temporal/contextual reference resolution. Smart risk detection: mutation_intent, cardinality_risk, for_each_detected. Automatic semantic expansion of related domains via the type registry.

routing.title: 🧭 Semantic Routing & Local Embeddings
routing.description: Intelligent router with multilingual E5 embeddings (100+ languages, ~5ms). Automatic semantic expansion of related domains (weather → location, calendar → contacts). Dynamically filtered capabilities catalog (96% token reduction). Adaptive HARD/SOFT/FALLBACK thresholds for optimal precision.

query.title: 🔍 Query Analysis
query.description: Semantic query analysis engine: intent extraction, named entity identification, temporal and spatial constraint detection, and action type classification (read, create, modify, delete). Support for ambiguous queries with automatic clarification requests.

planning.title: 📋 Intelligent Planning & FOR_EACH
planning.description: Advanced planner with execution DSL. FOR_EACH pattern for bulk iterations ('Send an email to all my contacts in group X'). Dependency graphs with automatic parallel execution. PANIC mode with adaptive retry. Golden Patterns (~100 pre-trained models) for optimal planning from the first try.

semanticTypes.title: 📦 Semantic Type System
semanticTypes.description: 26 semantic types organized in DAG hierarchy (inspired by schema.org/RDF/SKOS). Categories: Identity, Location, Temporal, ResourceID, Content, Measurement, Status. Central TypeRegistry with O(1) lookups, Wu & Palmer distance calculation, subsumption validation. Provenance tracking (source_domains, used_in_tools) for intelligent cross-domain expansion.

semanticValidation.title: ✅ Semantic Plan Validation
semanticValidation.description: LLM detection of 15 types of anomalies before execution: incomplete plan, missing tool, wrong parameters, logical incoherence, impossible constraint, temporal paradox, excessive cost, empty result, missing dependency, conflict between steps, ambiguous reference, unsupported format, exceeded capacity, unjustified privilege escalation, circular dependency. Criticality analysis (LOW/MEDIUM/HIGH) based on reversibility. Bayesian Pattern Learning integration (bypass >90% confidence). P95 < 2s with short-circuit for trivial plans.

semantic.title: 🎯 Semantic Validation & Pivot
semantic.description: Semantic validation of plans before execution to detect logical inconsistencies. Semantic pivot system allowing automatic reformulation of failed queries with alternative strategies (synonyms, criteria broadening, decomposition).

context.title: 🧠 Advanced Context Management
context.description: LIA resolves explicit references ('the first email') and implicit ones ('send him a message') through an entity resolution engine. The system maintains a typed data registry (emails, contacts, events) for automatic disambiguation with user suggestions when needed.

memory.title: 💾 Dual Short/Long-Term Memory
memory.description: Short-term: sliding message window with automatic context summarization. Long-term: extraction of facts about you (preferences, relationships, habits) stored with E5 embeddings. Hybrid BM25 + semantic search for optimal recall: both exact keywords AND meaning are used to retrieve your relevant memories.

interests.title: ✨ Smart Interest Learning
interests.description: Automatic learning of your interests through LLM conversation analysis. Proactive personalized notifications on your favorite topics via Wikipedia, Perplexity, or AI reflection. Bayesian weighting with temporal decay. Semantic deduplication via E5 embeddings. Full control: feedback, blocking, configurable time window and frequency.

summarization.title: 📝 Automatic Summarization
summarization.description: Intelligent summarization system for long conversations to maintain context without exceeding token limits. Key information extraction, semantic compression, and contextual injection into prompts to preserve conversational continuity.

hitl.title: 👤 Advanced Human-in-the-Loop
hitl.description: 8 intelligent interaction types: clarification, entity disambiguation (ENTITY_DISAMBIGUATION), plan approval, tool confirmation, draft critique, edit confirmation (EDIT_CONFIRMATION), bulk operation confirmation (FOR_EACH), and destructive validation (mass deletions ≥3 items). Configurable thresholds and item preview before execution.

connectors.title: 🔌 Connectors & Registry
connectors.description: Modular architecture with connector registry (Google Calendar, Gmail, People, Tasks, Drive, Places, Routes, Apple iCloud Mail/Calendar/Contacts, Microsoft Outlook/Calendar/Contacts/To Do, OpenWeatherMap, Wikipedia, Perplexity, Brave Search). Each connector has declarative manifests, JSON schemas auto-generated from Python types, and unified OAuth/API-key/credential management.

streaming.title: ⚡ Streaming & Real-Time
streaming.description: SSE (Server-Sent Events) streaming responses with typed events: tokens, tool states, HITL requests, execution metadata. The frontend processes the stream progressively for immediate interactive experience.

llm.title: 🤖 Multi-Provider LLM & Pattern Learning
llm.description: Support for OpenAI (GPT-4, o1), Anthropic (Claude), Google Gemini, DeepSeek, Qwen, Perplexity, and local Ollama. Bayesian learning of planning patterns (≥90% confidence → LLM bypass). Average 89% token savings. Configurable profiles per task with real-time cost tracking.

security.title: 🔐 Multi-Layer Security
security.description: OAuth 2.0 with PKCE for Google services, distributed locking for token refresh, PII filtering in logs and traces, rate limiting per user/endpoint, complete user isolation, and encryption of sensitive data.

resilience.title: 🛡️ Performance & Token Optimization
resilience.description: Text Compaction (~97% reduction per entity after Jinja templates). Circuit Breaker for external APIs. Multi-level Redis cache. Message windowing (93% token reduction). OAuth Proactive Refresh (tokens refreshed in background). Distributed rate limiting with exponential retry.

voice.title: 🎙️ Full Voice Mode
voice.description: Bidirectional system: TTS (free Edge or OpenAI/Gemini HD) for LIA to speak to you, and STT (offline Sherpa Whisper) to speak to LIA. Activate voice mode by saying 'OK Guy' or 'OK Guys' (wake word), or tap the microphone button. Automatic end-of-speech detection (VAD). Multilingual transcription (99+ languages). Completely free and offline on server side.

reminders.title: 🔔 Reminders & Push Notifications
reminders.description: Personal reminder system with multi-device FCM (Firebase Cloud Messaging) push notifications. Natural language creation, automatic timezone conversion, intelligent reference resolution ('next one', number, content match). Messages personalized with active personality and user memories.

personalities.title: 🎭 10+ Dynamic Personalities
personalities.description: 10+ pre-configured personalities (Normal, Enthusiastic, Professor, Friend, Influencer, Philosopher, Cynic, Poet, Coach...) with prompts translated in 6 languages. Each personality defines the assistant's tone, style and behavior. Full CRUD system: administrators can create, edit, enable/disable personalities. Per-user selection with automatic translation into their language.

geolocation.title: 📍 Smart Geolocation
geolocation.description: Dual location system: configurable home address in preferences and real-time position via browser Geolocation API. Used for place searches, local weather, and distance calculations. Consent management and automatic fallback.

i18n.title: 🌍 Complete Internationalization
i18n.description: 6 supported languages (FR, EN, ES, DE, IT, ZH) with: interface translation (i18next), localized dates (relative and absolute formats), agent responses in user language, gettext translations for backend, and automatic browser language detection.

observability.title: 📊 Production-Ready Observability
observability.description: Full stack: Prometheus metrics (tokens, latency, errors, costs), Grafana dashboards (15+), Tempo distributed tracing, Loki centralized logs, and Langfuse integration for LLM performance analysis. Configurable alerts with per-environment thresholds.

tools.title: 🔧 50+ Typed Tools
tools.description: Over 50 native tools with JSON schemas auto-generated from Python/Pydantic, infinitely extensible via MCP servers. Declarative manifests with semantic_keywords for intelligent routing. Unified StandardToolOutput with registry updates for contextual references.

costTransparency.title: 💰 Cost Transparency
costTransparency.description: Optional real-time display: input/output/cache tokens, EUR cost per message and conversation total. Dashboard statistics: messages, tokens, and costs for current cycle. User control via 💲 icon in title bar.

scheduledActions.title: 📅 Scheduled Actions
scheduledActions.description: Recurring actions executed automatically by the assistant on a defined schedule. Configure days of the week and a time, attach a prompt instruction and LIA will execute it automatically. Results archived in conversation and notified via push. Auto-disable after 5 consecutive failures.

mcp.title: 🔌 MCP (Model Context Protocol)
mcp.description: Full MCP protocol support with admin and per-user servers. 4 authentication types (none, API key, bearer token, OAuth 2.1). Automatic tool discovery at connection test, LLM-generated domain descriptions for intelligent routing, and read_me content injection into the planner prompt for context-aware tool usage.

mcpApps.title: 📱 Interactive MCP Apps
mcpApps.description: Sandboxed HTML widgets rendered directly in the conversation via secure iframes. JSON-RPC PostMessage bridge for bidirectional communication. Built-in XSS protection and COEP credentialless policy for safe loading of external resources.

excalidraw.title: 🎨 Excalidraw Generation
excalidraw.description: LLM-driven diagram generation with structured intent mode: a single LLM call generates all elements (shapes and arrows) for precise visual output. Supports architecture diagrams, workflows, org charts and more. Raw elements fallback with automatic position correction (overlap fixing and centering).

multichannel.title: 📲 Multi-Channel (Telegram)
multichannel.description: Generic multi-channel messaging architecture with Telegram as first implementation. Telegram bot with OTP binding for secure account linking. Voice message support with automatic transcription. Async webhook processing for reliable message handling.

heartbeatAutonome.title: 💓 Autonomous Heartbeat
heartbeatAutonome.description: LLM-driven proactive contextual notifications with 8 parallel data sources aggregated in real-time. 2-phase approach: structured decision (skip/notify via cheap model) then personality-aware message rewrite. Weather change detection (rain start/end, temperature drops, wind alerts). Configurable time windows and per-user push control.

webFetch.title: 🌐 Intelligent Web Fetch
webFetch.description: Dedicated web content retrieval and extraction tool from any public URL, no API key required. Clean Markdown extraction via readability.js (articles, documentation, data). Multi-layer SSRF protection: DNS validation, post-redirect verification, content size limits, per-user rate limiting. Ideal for importing articles, documentation pages, or structured data directly into the conversation.

knowledgeEnrichment.title: 📰 Brave Search Contextual Enrichment
knowledgeEnrichment.description: Automatic response enrichment via Brave Search (Web + News). Before formulating its response, LIA searches for up-to-date data to contextualize and complement its knowledge. Smart Redis caching with global fallback to optimize API calls. Personal per-user Brave API key (configured in Settings > Connectors). Enables always up-to-date responses: news, prices, schedules, ongoing events, and more.

adaptiveReplanner.title: 🔄 Adaptive Replanning
adaptiveReplanner.description: Intelligent autonomous recovery from execution failures (empty results, partial failures, dependency errors, semantic mismatches). Rule-based analysis without additional LLM calls for speed. Recovery strategies: retry with adjusted parameters, skip non-critical steps, full re-planning if necessary. PANIC mode with exponential backoff. Users always receive the best possible response, even with partial failures.

parallelExecution.title: ⚡ Wave-by-Wave Parallel Execution
parallelExecution.description: Native parallel execution engine built on asyncio.gather(), replacing LangGraph's broken Command+Send pattern. Automatic execution wave calculation via dependency graph: independent steps run simultaneously, dependent steps wait for prerequisites. Result: complex multi-domain plans (weather + routes + places + calendar) executed in seconds instead of sequentially.

dataRegistry.title: 🗂️ Data Registry & Rich Rendering
dataRegistry.description: Unified structured data system for rich display in conversations. 15+ registry types (Contact, Email, Event, Task, File, Place, Route, Weather, MCP App...). Immutable items with metadata, TTL, and deterministic IDs. Sent via SSE before the LLM response for instant card rendering. HTML template engine with multi-domain, hierarchical support, and automatic correlation between related results (e.g., event + route grouped together).

qualityAssurance.title: 🎯 LLM-as-Judge Quality Assurance
qualityAssurance.description: Automated response quality evaluation pipeline. Three specialized LLM evaluators: relevance (query/response adequacy), hallucination (fabricated content detection), latency (performance SLA compliance). Metrics exported to Prometheus and Langfuse for production monitoring. Real-time identification and correction of quality degradations.

attachments.title: 📎 Photos & File Attachments
attachments.description: Photo and PDF document upload via 📎 button or drag & drop, with automatic client-side compression. Visual analysis via configurable vision LLM (gpt-4.1-mini by default). PDF text extraction via PyMuPDF. Inline thumbnails in messages, accessible lightbox. Strict per-user isolation, automatic cleanup (conversation reset + 24h TTL). "Reference + Late Resolution" architecture for lightweight checkpoints.

skills.title: Skills
skills.description: Supercharge LIA with custom expert skills for your business or personal needs. Create your own skills in natural language with the built-in Skill Generator — or import from agentskills.io-compatible marketplaces. Compatible with 30+ tools including Claude Code, Cursor, and VS Code.

ragSpaces.title: 📚 RAG Knowledge Spaces
ragSpaces.description: Create personal knowledge spaces and upload your own documents (PDF, TXT, MD, DOCX). Documents are automatically processed (text extraction, chunking, OpenAI embedding). Active spaces enrich assistant responses via hybrid search combining semantic similarity (pgvector cosine) and BM25 keyword matching. Full cost transparency with per-document and per-query embedding tracking. Admin reindexation endpoint for embedding model changes.

subAgents.title: Sub-Agents
subAgents.description: Delegate complex tasks to specialized ephemeral experts working in parallel. The assistant orchestrates and synthesizes their findings transparently.

browserControl.title: 🌐 Browser Control
browserControl.description: Interactive web browsing with autonomous navigation: search products on e-commerce sites, fill forms, extract data from JavaScript-rendered pages. The browser agent handles multi-step interactions automatically.

getting_started.title: Getting Started
getting_started.description: How to get started with LIA

q1.question: What is LIA?
q1.answer:
LIA (Learning Intelligent Assistant) is your personal intelligent assistant powered by artificial intelligence. LIA helps you daily with:

• Managing your emails: search, read, send, reply and forward
• Organizing your calendar: create, modify and view your events
• Managing your contacts: search, create and update
• Managing your tasks: create, track and complete your to-dos
• Exploring your files: search and read your Drive documents
• Getting information: weather, Wikipedia, web search, places

LIA understands natural language: talk to it like you would a human assistant!

q2.question: How do I start a conversation with LIA?
q2.answer:
It's simple in 3 steps:

1. Click on Chat in the left menu
2. Type your message in the input box at the bottom of the screen
3. Press Enter to send

💡 Tip: Be natural! LIA understands phrases like "Show me my emails from this week" or "What do I have scheduled tomorrow?"

q3.question: Does LIA support multiple languages?
q3.answer:
Yes! LIA supports 6 languages:

🇫🇷 French (Français)
🇬🇧 English
🇪🇸 Spanish (Español)
🇩🇪 German (Deutsch)
🇮🇹 Italian (Italiano)
🇨🇳 Simplified Chinese (简体中文)

To change language:
• Click the globe icon 🌐 in the title bar
• Or go to Settings > Appearance > Language

LIA will respond in the interface language.

q5.question: How does the approval system work?
q5.answer:
To protect your data, LIA uses an intelligent approval system (Human-in-the-Loop). Before any action that modifies your data, LIA asks for confirmation:

🔒 Actions requiring approval:
• Send, reply to or forward an email
• Create, modify or delete an event
• Create, modify or delete a contact
• Create or complete a task

📋 How it works:
1. LIA prepares a draft of the action
2. You see a detailed preview of what will be done
3. You can:
• ✅ Approve: the action is executed
• ✏️ Edit: correct before validation
• ❌ Cancel: nothing is done

💡 Tip: Take time to review the preview, especially for emails!

q6.question: Can LIA make mistakes?
q6.answer:
Like any AI assistant, LIA may sometimes misinterpret a request. Here's how to get the best results:

✅ Best practices:
• Be specific: "Send an email to john.smith@email.com" rather than "Send an email to John"
• Give context: "Find my emails from this week about the Alpha project"
• Review previews before approving actions

⚠️ If there's an error:
• Rephrase your request with more details
• Use specific dates ("January 15") rather than relative ones ("last week")
• For important emails, always review the preview

🛡️ Built-in security:
Thanks to the approval system, no critical action is executed without your explicit validation.

q1.question: Can I have multiple conversations at the same time?
q1.answer:
LIA works with one active conversation. This design choice allows:

• A coherent context: LIA remembers the entire conversation
• Natural references: "the first email", "the previous contact"
• A smooth experience: no need to juggle between windows

To start fresh:
Click the New conversation button (🗑️ icon). Warning: this deletes the current history.

q2.question: How do I reset a conversation?
q2.answer:
To clear history and start over:

1. In the chat, find the button with the 🗑️ icon in the header
2. Click it
3. Confirm deletion

⚠️ Warning: This action is irreversible. All conversation history will be permanently deleted.

💡 When to reset?
• When you completely change topics
• If LIA seems confused by previous context
• For privacy reasons

q3.question: Are conversations saved?
q3.answer:
Yes, your conversations are automatically saved:

• Real-time sync: each message is recorded instantly
• Multi-device: access your conversation from any device
• Persistence: find your conversation even after logging out

📱 Multi-device tip:
You can start a conversation on your computer and continue on mobile (and vice-versa).

q4.question: How do I use line breaks in my messages?
q4.answer:
Two keyboard shortcuts to know:

• Enter = Send the message
• Shift + Enter = New line (line break)

💡 Practical example:
To send a structured email, type:

Send an email to John:
Subject: Project meeting
Body:
Hello John,

I confirm our meeting tomorrow.

Best regards

Use Shift+Enter between each line, then Enter to send.

q5.question: Does LIA remember the conversation context?
q5.answer:
Yes! LIA maintains an intelligent context throughout the conversation:

🔗 Contextual references:
• "Show my emails from John" → finds 3 emails
• "Reply to the second one" → LIA knows you're talking about the 2nd email
• "Add Sophie in CC" → LIA remembers the draft in progress

📋 What LIA remembers:
• Previous search results
• Contacts, emails, events mentioned
• Drafts awaiting approval
• The conversation thread

💡 Tip: Use natural references like "the first one", "Mary's one", "tomorrow's appointment".

q6.question: Why does LIA sometimes take time to respond?
q6.answer:
Response time varies based on your request's complexity:

⚡ Fast responses (1-3 seconds):
• Simple conversation questions
• Weather information
• Wikipedia searches

⏱️ Medium responses (3-10 seconds):
• Searching your emails
• Calendar consultation
• Contact search

🔄 Longer responses (10-30 seconds):
• Creating complex emails
• Combined multiple searches
• Actions requiring multiple steps

💡 Visual indicators:
During processing, you'll see messages like "🔍 Analyzing your request..." or "📋 Preparing action plan..."

q7.question: How do I get better results with LIA?
q7.answer:
Here are the best practices for communicating with LIA:

✅ Be specific:
❌ "Find an email"
✅ "Find emails from Peter about the budget"

✅ Give context:
❌ "Send an email"
✅ "Send an email to mary@example.com to confirm the meeting tomorrow at 2pm"

✅ Use specific dates:
❌ "The meeting the other day"
✅ "The meeting on January 15"

✅ Proceed step by step:
For complex tasks, break down into multiple requests.

✅ Check previews:
Before approving an email or modification, carefully review the preview.

q8.question: How do I attach photos or documents to my messages?
q8.answer:
Two ways to attach files:

📎 Paperclip button:
Click the 📎 button to the left of the input area to browse your files.

🖱️ Drag and drop:
Drag files directly from your file explorer into the input area. A visual outline appears to confirm the drop zone.

📁 Accepted formats:
• Photos (JPEG, PNG, GIF, WebP, HEIC) — automatically compressed
• PDF documents — text is automatically extracted

Thumbnails are displayed in the conversation. Click a thumbnail to enlarge it. LIA can analyze the visual content of images and the text of PDFs to answer your questions.

💡 Limits: maximum 5 attachments per message, 10 MB per image, 20 MB per document.

q9.question: What is the /resume command?
q9.answer:
The /resume command triggers intelligent context compaction:

What it does:
• Summarizes old conversation history using AI
• Preserves critical identifiers (names, emails, IDs)
• Frees up context window space for longer conversations

When to use it:
• When your conversation has been going on for a long time
• When LIA seems to lose track of earlier context
• When you want to "reset" the context without losing important information

How it works:
1. Type /resume in the chat
2. LIA summarizes old messages into a concise recap
3. Recent messages are preserved intact
4. LIA confirms the compaction with a brief summary

💡 Note: Compaction also triggers automatically when the conversation becomes very long. The /resume command lets you force it at any time.

q7.question: How do I enable or disable LIA's voice?

q8.question: How do I change LIA's voice (male/female)?

q9.question: How does long-term memory work?
q9.answer:
LIA learns about you through its intelligent memory:

🧠 What LIA remembers:
• Preferences: "He prefers concise emails", "She likes black coffee"
• Personal information: "His birthday is March 15"
• Relationships: "Mary is his sister", "John is his colleague"
• Significant events: "He changed jobs recently"
• Habits: "She exercises on Tuesday evenings"
• Sensitive areas: topics to approach with care

🔄 Automatic extraction:
LIA analyzes your conversations and automatically extracts relevant information in the background.

🔍 Hybrid search:
LIA uses hybrid search combining keywords (BM25) and meaning (semantic) to retrieve your relevant memories with optimal accuracy.

📊 Usage:
These memories personalize LIA's responses. For example, LIA will say "Hello Mary" to your sister without you specifying.

🛡️ Full control:
View, edit, pin or delete your memories in Settings > Memory.

q10.question: How do I manage my stored memories?
q10.answer:
You have full control over what LIA remembers:

📋 View your memories:
Go to Settings > Long-term Memory to see everything LIA has remembered.

✏️ Edit a memory:
• Click the edit button
• Correct the content or category
• Adjust usage nuances

📌 Pin a memory:
Pinned memories are protected from automatic cleanup. Useful for important information.

🗑️ Delete:
• Delete individually or in bulk
• Option to keep pinned memories

📤 Export:
Export all your memories for backup (GDPR compliance).

🔄 Automatic cleanup:
Rarely used and unpinned memories are automatically purged after 6 months to keep the system relevant.

q11.question: How do I configure my geolocation and home address?

q12.question: How does LIA resolve relational references?
q12.answer:
LIA understands references to your loved ones through its memory:

👥 Reference examples:
• "Send an email to my brother" → LIA finds the corresponding contact
• "What did my wife send me?" → searches your emails
• "Call my doctor" → finds Dr. X's contact

🔗 How it works:
1. LIA extracts relationships from your conversations ("My brother's name is John")
2. These associations are stored in your long-term memory
3. During a query, LIA automatically resolves "my brother" to "John Smith"

✏️ Customize relationships:
You can create or modify these associations in Settings > Long-term Memory.

💡 Tip:
Mention your relationships naturally in conversations: "My colleague Sophie sent me a file".

q13.question: How do I view my token consumption in chat?
q13.answer:
You can display consumption metrics in real-time during your conversations:

💰 To enable/disable display:
Click the dollar icon 💲 in the title bar (available on desktop only).

📊 Information displayed:
When enabled, each LIA message shows:
• Input tokens: your message + analyzed context
• Output tokens: the response generated by LIA
• Cached tokens: reused data (savings)
• Estimated cost: in euros for this message
• Conversation total: cumulative since start

💡 Why is this useful?
• Understand what consumes resources
• Optimize your requests if needed
• Track your usage in real-time

📱 Note:
This feature is not available on mobile due to display space constraints.

q14.question: Which languages are supported for voice transcription?

q15.question: Voice mode isn't working, what should I do?
q15.answer:
Here are solutions to common voice mode issues:

🎤 Microphone problem:
1. Check that your browser has permission to use the microphone
2. Click the 🔒 icon to the left of the URL to see permissions
3. Allow the microphone if blocked

🌐 Unsupported browser:
Voice mode requires a modern browser:
• ✅ Chrome 92+
• ✅ Firefox 79+
• ✅ Safari 15.2+
• ✅ Edge 92+
• ❌ Internet Explorer (not supported)

🔒 Security headers:
Voice mode uses advanced features (SharedArrayBuffer) that require security headers. If you see a "crossOriginIsolated" error, contact the administrator.

🔇 Wake word not detected:
• Say "OK Guy" or "OK Guys" clearly
• Avoid noisy environments
• Get closer to the microphone
• Use Push-to-Talk (click the overlay) as an alternative

⏱️ Slow transcription:
The first transcription may take 2-3 seconds (model loading). Subsequent ones are faster (~500ms).

q1.question: What is a connector?
q1.answer:
A connector is a secure gateway between LIA and your external services:

🔌 Types of connectors:

Google Services (OAuth):
• 📧 Gmail: read, send, reply to emails
• 📅 Calendar: manage your events
• 👥 Contacts: search and create contacts
• 📁 Drive: explore your files
• ✅ Tasks: manage your tasks
• 📍 Places: search for locations
• 🗺️ Routes: directions and routes

Apple iCloud services (app-specific password):
• 📮 Apple Mail: read, send, reply to emails (IMAP/SMTP)
• 🗓️ Apple Calendar: manage your iCloud events (CalDAV)
• 📇 Apple Contacts: search and create iCloud contacts (CardDAV)

Microsoft 365 services (OAuth):
• 📧 Outlook: read, send, reply to emails
• 📅 Microsoft Calendar: manage your events
• 👥 Microsoft Contacts: search and manage contacts
• ✅ Microsoft To Do: manage tasks and lists

External services (API key):
• 🌤️ OpenWeatherMap: weather and forecasts
• 📚 Wikipedia: encyclopedic search
• 🔍 Perplexity: AI web search
• 🦁 Brave Search: web and news enrichment

Smart Home (hybrid auth):
• 💡 Philips Hue: control lights, rooms, and scenes

🔒 Security:
Each connector has limited permissions. LIA only accesses necessary data.

⚡ Mutual exclusivity:
For email, calendar, and contacts, only one provider can be active at a time (Google OR Apple OR Microsoft, not both simultaneously). Tasks are available via Google Tasks or Microsoft To Do (one at a time).

q2.question: How do I connect Google services?
q2.answer:
Google services use OAuth 2.0 (secure connection):

Steps:
1. Go to Settings > Connectors
2. Find the desired service (Gmail, Calendar, etc.)
3. Click Connect
4. A Google window opens
5. Select your Google account
6. Accept the requested permissions
7. You're redirected to LIA - the connector is active!

🔐 Permissions:
LIA only requests necessary permissions. For example, for Gmail: reading and sending emails, but not permanent deletion.

💡 Tip:
You can revoke access at any time from LIA settings or from your Google account.

q3.question: How do I connect external services (API key)?
q3.answer:
Some services require a personal API key:

🌤️ OpenWeatherMap (free):
1. Create an account at openweathermap.org
2. Get your free API key
3. In LIA: Settings > Connectors > OpenWeatherMap
4. Paste your key and click Activate

🔍 Perplexity (paid):
1. Create an account at perplexity.ai
2. Subscribe to an API plan
3. Get your API key
4. Activate in LIA as above

📚 Wikipedia:
No key required! Wikipedia is free and directly accessible.

🔒 Key security:
Your API keys are stored securely and encrypted.

q4.question: How do I disconnect a service?
q4.answer:
To revoke LIA's access to a service:

Disconnection from LIA:
1. Go to Settings > Connectors
2. Find the service to disconnect
3. Click Disconnect
4. Confirm disconnection

⚠️ Consequences:
• LIA will no longer be able to access this service
• Your data remains intact with the provider (Google, etc.)
• You can reconnect at any time

🔐 Double security (Google):
For Google services, you can also revoke access from:
• myaccount.google.com/permissions
• This disconnects LIA immediately

q5.question: Why is a connector showing an error?
q5.answer:
Several reasons can explain a connector error:

🔄 Expired token:
Solution: Click Reconnect to refresh the authorization.

🚫 Permissions revoked:
If you revoked access from Google, reconnect.

🔑 Invalid API key:
For API key services, verify your key is correct and active.

⏱️ Quota exceeded:
Some services have usage limits. Wait or upgrade to a higher plan.

🌐 Network issue:
Check your internet connection and try again.

💡 General solution:
Disconnect then reconnect the service. This solves most problems.

q6.question: Can I configure preferences per connector?
q6.answer:
Yes! Some connectors have customizable preferences:

📅 Google Calendar:
• Default calendar: where to create new events
• Example: "Work", "Personal", "Family"
• LIA will use this calendar unless specified otherwise

✅ Google Tasks:
• Default task list: where to create new tasks
• Example: "My main list", "Projects", "Shopping"

📍 Google Places:
• Home address: your default address for "near me" searches
• Automatic geolocation: send your position for accurate results
• Configure both in Settings > Connectors > Google Places

🌐 Browser Geolocation:
• In Google Places settings, enable the geolocation toggle
• Your browser will ask for permission the first time
• Position used for local weather and "near me" searches

To configure:
1. Go to Settings (⚙️ icon in the menu)
2. Open the Connectors section
3. Find the relevant connector (Calendar, Tasks, or Places)
4. Configure the specific preferences for that connector

💡 Tip:
These preferences save you time by not having to specify the calendar, list, or city each time.

q8.question: Are connectors secure?
q8.answer:
Security is our top priority:

🔐 Google services — OAuth 2.0:
• LIA never sees your Google passwords
• You connect directly with Google
• Tokens revocable at any time

🍎 Apple services — App-specific password:
• Uses an application-specific password (not your Apple password)
• Encrypted before database storage
• Revocable from appleid.apple.com
• Apple two-factor authentication is required

🪟 Microsoft 365 — OAuth 2.0:
• LIA never sees your Microsoft passwords
• You connect directly with Microsoft
• Tokens revocable at any time from your Microsoft account
• Limited permissions (Microsoft Graph API)

🔒 Minimal permissions:
• Each connector only requests necessary permissions
• No excessive access to your data

🗄️ Secure storage:
• OAuth tokens and app-specific passwords encrypted in database
• API keys stored securely
• No plaintext storage

🔄 Automatic refresh:
• Google and Microsoft tokens expire regularly and are renewed automatically
• Apple app-specific passwords don't expire (unless Apple ID password changes)

📋 Audit and logs:
• All actions are tracked
• Usage history available

q9.question: How do I connect Apple iCloud services?
q9.answer:
Apple iCloud services use an app-specific password:

Prerequisites:
• An Apple account with two-factor authentication enabled
• Generate an app-specific password from appleid.apple.com

Steps:
1. Go to Settings > Connectors
2. In the Apple iCloud section, click Connect
3. Enter your Apple ID (email)
4. Enter the app-specific password (format xxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxx)
5. Select the services to activate (Email, Calendar, Contacts)
6. Click Test connection to verify
7. Click Activate

⚠️ Mutual exclusivity:
Activating an Apple service automatically deactivates its Google equivalent (and vice versa). For example, activating Apple Mail will deactivate Gmail.

🔐 Security:
Your app-specific password is encrypted and never exposed. You can revoke it at any time from your Apple account.

q10.question: What Apple iCloud services can I connect?
q10.answer:
LIA integrates with 3 Apple iCloud services:

📮 Apple Mail (IMAP/SMTP)
• Search your iCloud emails
• Read message content
• Send, reply, forward
• View attachments
• Navigate by IMAP folders

🗓️ Apple Calendar (CalDAV)
• View your iCloud calendar
• Create events
• Modify or delete
• Invite participants

📇 Apple Contacts (CardDAV)
• Search by name, email, company
• Create new contacts
• Update information

❌ Not available:
• Apple Reminders: Apple doesn't provide a standard API for accessing Reminders from a server. Tasks remain available via Google Tasks or Microsoft To Do.

💡 Same experience:
Commands are identical whether you use Google or Apple. Just say "my emails", "my calendar", or "my contacts" — LIA automatically uses the active provider.

q11.question: What is connector mutual exclusivity?
q11.answer:
For email, calendar, contacts, and tasks services, LIA applies a mutual exclusivity rule:

⚡ Principle:
Only one provider can be active per category:
• Email: Gmail OR Apple Mail OR Outlook (not multiple)
• Calendar: Google Calendar OR Apple Calendar OR Microsoft Calendar (not multiple)
• Contacts: Google Contacts OR Apple Contacts OR Microsoft Contacts (not multiple)
• Tasks: Google Tasks OR Microsoft To Do (not both)

🔄 Switching providers:
If you activate a new provider while another is active for the same category:
1. The previous service becomes inactive (not deleted)
2. The new service becomes active
3. Your data remains intact with each provider
4. You can switch back at any time

✅ Unaffected services:
Google Drive, Places, Routes, and Philips Hue have no equivalent in other providers and remain always available.

💡 Tip:
You don't need to choose the same provider for everything. For example, you can use Outlook for emails, Google Calendar for your calendar, and Microsoft To Do for tasks.

q12.question: How do I connect Microsoft 365 services?
q12.answer:
Microsoft 365 services use OAuth 2.0 (secure connection via Microsoft Identity Platform):

Steps:
1. Go to Settings > Connectors
2. In the Microsoft 365 section, click Connect All or select an individual service
3. A Microsoft login window opens
4. Sign in with your Microsoft account
5. Accept the requested permissions
6. You're redirected back to LIA — the connector is active!

🔐 Permissions:
LIA only requests necessary permissions via the Microsoft Graph API. For example, for Outlook: reading and sending emails, but not permanent deletion.

⚠️ Mutual exclusivity:
Activating a Microsoft service automatically deactivates its Google or Apple equivalent for the same category (and vice versa).

💡 Tip:
You can revoke access at any time from LIA settings or from account.microsoft.com.

q13.question: What Microsoft 365 services can I connect?
q13.answer:
LIA integrates with 4 Microsoft 365 services:

📧 Outlook (Microsoft Graph)
• Search your emails
• Read message content
• Send, reply, forward
• View attachments

📅 Microsoft Calendar
• View your schedule
• Create events
• Modify or delete
• Invite participants

👥 Microsoft Contacts
• Search by name, email, company
• Create new contacts
• Update information

✅ Microsoft To Do
• List your tasks
• Create new tasks
• Mark as completed
• Manage task lists

💡 Same experience:
Commands are identical regardless of provider. Just say "my emails", "my calendar", "my contacts", or "my tasks" — LIA automatically uses the active provider.

q14.question: How do I connect Philips Hue?
q14.answer:
Philips Hue uses hybrid authentication (local press-link or remote OAuth2):

🏠 Local mode (recommended — same network):
1. Go to Settings > Smart Home > Philips Hue
2. Click Local connection
3. Click Search for bridges
4. Select your bridge
5. Press the physical button on top of your Hue Bridge
6. Click Pair within 30 seconds
7. LIA validates connectivity and activates the connector

🌐 Remote mode (different network):
1. Configure Hue Remote API credentials on the server
2. Click Remote connection
3. Authorize LIA via Philips OAuth2

💡 What you can do:
• "Turn on the living room lights"
• "Dim the bedroom to 30%"
• "Set the kitchen light to blue"
• "Activate the movie scene"
• "What lights are currently on?"
• "Turn off everything"

⚡ 6 tools available:
List lights, control a light (on/off, brightness, color), list rooms, control a room, list scenes, activate a scene.

q1.question: How do I search my contacts?

q2.question: How do I create or modify a contact?

🗑️ Delete a contact:

q3.question: How do I search my emails?
q3.answer:
Gmail - Powerful search:

🔍 Simple search:
• "Show my unread emails"
• "Find emails from Amazon"
• "Emails from this week"

🔍 Advanced search:
• "Emails from Peter about the budget"

q4.question: How do I read an email in detail?

Gmail - Full content display:

q5.question: How do I send an email?

Gmail - Sending emails with preview:

📤 Structured send:

📤 With CC:

✅ Sending process:

q6.question: How do I reply to or forward an email?

↪️ Forward:

• The thread is preserved
• Attachments are included (forward)

💡 Tip:
After a search, use references like "the first one", "John's one" to designate the email.

q7.question: How do I delete an email?
q7.answer:
Gmail - Secure deletion:

🗑️ Delete an email:
• "Delete the spam email from yesterday"

2. You see the sender, subject and date
